name: WP-E15-typed-errors: typed errors + classification fixes at API boundaries
description: Fix server-side error typing and classification. Findings: F-015 (system-prompt/events/flag handlers throw raw Error('Session not found') -> 500 instead of typed 404) · F-016 (vocabulary review catch-all misclassifies DB errors as 422 and echoes raw err.message to the client) · F-017 (JWKS response shape unvalidated — a malformed 200 is misclassified as a token error -> 401/sign-out; touches the Clerk auth path, see project memory on Clerk JWKS fallback). Use the shared typed error hierarchy in @eduagent/schemas (UX Resilience Rules); classify raw errors before formatting (Code Quality Guards). Full finding text: docs/audit/2026-05-29-full-audit/L-gap-delta.md (label errors-api).
acceptanceCriteria: Raw Error throws in the cited handlers replaced with typed errors mapping to correct status codes; DB errors no longer surface as 422 nor echo raw messages; JWKS response shape validated before classification with a break test for the malformed-200 case.
riskImpact: P2 but medium-supervision: F-017 sits on the Clerk JWKS auth path — a wrong fix could cause wrongful sign-outs fleet-wide or regress the documented Clerk token-template fallback. Mitigation: response-shape validation only (no auth-flow shape change without operator review before merge); break test for the malformed-200 case; fallback behavior regression-tested. F-015/F-016 are low-risk classification corrections.
bodyText:
Bundle brief — WP-E15-typed-errors (WP DoR bridge, PRG-15)
Rationale: three misclassification/typing findings on neighboring API boundaries, bundled because they share one fix discipline: classify the RAW error first via the shared typed error hierarchy in @eduagent/schemas, then map to the correct status/response (UX Resilience Rules + Code Quality Guards). Runs serially after WP-E15-catch-hygiene (WI-639, merged f54dd3c0f) — same neighborhood, sequenced to avoid surface collisions.
Strategy: one PR, three clusters. (1) F-015: replace raw Error('Session not found') throws in system-prompt/events/flag handlers with the typed not-found error -> 404, killing the spurious 500s + Sentry noise. (2) F-016: vocabulary review route catch-all — classify DB/transient errors correctly (5xx, retryable) instead of blanket 422, and stop echoing raw err.message to the client. (3) F-017: validate the JWKS response shape before classification so a malformed upstream 200 is treated as an upstream/transient failure, NOT a token error — no wrongful 401/sign-out, no poisoned cache.
MEDIUM SUPERVISION FLAG (operator-ratified): F-017 touches the Clerk JWKS auth path. The documented Clerk token-template fallback behavior (API falls back to Clerk Backend API when token-template drift omits email_verified — see .claude/memory/project_clerk_email_verification_fallback.md) must NOT regress. If the fix changes the SHAPE of the auth flow (beyond adding response-shape validation), the shepherd surfaces the design to the operator BEFORE merge.
Scope — findings absorbed (full text: docs/audit/2026-05-29-full-audit/L-gap-delta.md, label errors-api)
F-015 — system-prompt/events/flag handlers throw raw Error('Session not found') -> 500 + spurious Sentry instead of typed 404 (errors-api-critic-1; deepsec-BUG-other-error-handling-72f9b181bc).
F-016 — vocabulary review route catch-all misclassifies transient DB errors as 422 and echoes raw err.message to client (errors-api-critic-2; deepsec-BUG-other-error-misclassification-4fb294fb99).
F-017 — jwt.ts JWKS response shape unvalidated: malformed upstream 200 misclassified as token error -> wrongful 401/sign-out + poisoned cache (errors-api-critic-3; deepsec-BUG-other-error-misclassification-d0408c2485).
Bundle Acceptance Criteria
F-015 handlers throw typed errors from the shared hierarchy mapping to 404; no raw Error('Session not found') remains in the cited handlers; regression test asserts 404 (not 500) for a missing session.
F-016: transient/DB failures in the vocabulary review route surface as the correct 5xx class and never echo raw err.message to the client; 422 is reserved for genuine validation failures; regression tests cover both paths (validation error -> 422, DB error -> 5xx, no raw message leak).
F-017: JWKS fetch validates response shape before classification; a malformed-200 break test demonstrates no wrongful 401/sign-out and no caching of the malformed payload; the documented Clerk token-template fallback behavior is regression-tested or demonstrably untouched.
All classification happens on the raw error object before any formatting; no string-matching on formatted output (Code Quality Guards).
Red-green evidence recorded per cluster (test RED against pre-fix code, GREEN after).
Provenance: 2 thin children carry absorbed provenance; lifecycle rides this parent — children are never claimed/executed standalone (ZDX-ADR-0001 pattern, IF bridge ruling 2026-06-10 extended to PRG-15).
